name: Berberine improved aged rat cognition, muscle markers, and AMPK-linked signaling
summary: Berberine has an animal functional and AMPK/SIRT1/PGC-1alpha pathway signal but no staged human aging evidence in this pass.
statement: In naturally aging rats, six months of berberine improved cognitive deficits, insulin resistance, skeletal-muscle morphology, serum and muscle ROS, skeletal-muscle ATP, and p-AMPK, SIRT1, and PGC-1alpha protein expression.
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports p<0.01 for several cognitive, insulin-resistance, muscle, ROS, ATP, and pathway-marker comparisons but does not provide a single geroscience effect size.
caveats: This is rat evidence and does not establish human aging benefit.
The source links AMPK/SIRT1/PGC-1alpha signaling to muscle effects, but the intervention is pleiotropic.
No human berberine aging-biomarker or functional trial was staged in this baseline.
